DISCOVERY 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JAMES RYAN'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE WRITTEN RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM AND SPECIAL INTEROGGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO PRODUCE ALL REQUESTED DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT TO RESPOND TO OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS BY JANUARY 3, 2012. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE NOT WAIVED. THE COURT DID NOT AWARD SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS OOC INC., TODD PARENT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF JAMES RYAN'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO DEFENDANT TODD PARENT ONLY. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION PURPORTS TO STATE A CLAIM FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY AND THAT CLAIM IS ADEQUATELY PLED (COLLIER V. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 228 CAL. APP. 3D 1117), BUT, LIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACITON FOR LABOR CODE VIOLATIONS, ONLY LIES AGAINST AN EMPLOYER. THE SECOND AND TENTH CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION EXCLUSIVITY RULE BECAUSE OF THE ALLEGATIONS, LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THAT DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT CONTRAVENES FUNDAMENTAL CALIFORNIA POLICIES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS OOC INC., TODD PARENT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF JAMES RYAN'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearance by counsel (no card provided).
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled as to the second and tenth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the first and third cause of action as to defendant Todd Parent only. Liberally construed, the first cause of action purports to state a claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy and that claim is adequately pled (Collier v. Superior Court (1991) 228 Cal. App. 3d 1117), but, like the third cause of aciton for Labor Code violations, only lies against an employer. The second and tenth claims are not barred by the workers' compensation exclusivity rule because of the allegations, liberally construed, that defendants' conduct contravenes fundamental California policies. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported
